behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality from a real speech-to-text model (speaker diarization accuracy), not just local wiring. A mock STT response would trivially pass and hide real diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE). No real-OS-only requirement dominates, and this is one model decision class, not a multi-agentic chain. Rejected Workflow: behavior text checks STT label preservation, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. Matrix used: Agentic vs Workflow row settled this as single-step model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting recording through the real STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for attendee utterances used by downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields; minimum failure boundary is a local function/object transform (MOCK-MISS => Unit floor). No required real OS/network/subprocess and no dependence on model judgment. Rejected Integration: behavior text is extraction logic itself, not a module handoff-only failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript lines into the parsing function and assert exact assignee/task tuples in the summary-input structure, including edge cases (multiple assignees, punctuation, imperative phrasing).

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM extraction/coverage quality from transcript content; stubbed outputs would mask missing-attendee action items (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Not primarily OS/subprocess behavior. Rejected Workflow: one summary-generation decision is sufficient to observe this failure; no required multi-agentic chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real meeting transcript/audio with known assignments across multiple attendees, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing/mapping logic once decisions are identified; failure boundary is local transformation code. No real infra or model-output dependency is necessary to catch misrouting. Rejected Integration: behavior text is about routing logic, not cross-module state propagation as the only failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Given structured inputs containing identified decisions plus narrative text, assert routing function places decisions only in `decisions` schema field and not in generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model extraction/placement quality from real transcript semantics; deterministic fixtures can’t validate whether the model actually surfaces key decisions correctly (LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on one live summary output decision-placement outcome, not multiple agentic stages interacting.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript content with known key decisions through the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential segment-processing steps and merged coherence, with repeated real STT/LLM outputs influencing downstream merge quality (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Single-call Agentic is insufficient because failures can emerge only across segment-to-segment handoff/merge interactions. Matrix used: Agentic vs Workflow row settled this as multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute end-to-end segmented processing with real STT+LLM per segment, and assert sequential processing, complete coverage across segments, and coherent merged summary (no dropped/duplicated cross-segment decisions/actions).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real STT and LLM steps in sequence; correctness and completion depend on chained agentic stages plus orchestration. Lower levels would miss cross-stage failures and real model-driven breakpoints. Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly spans multiple steps (upload→transcribe→summarize→deliver), not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like real meeting recording through the full pipeline and assert autonomous completion, successful structured delivery, and valid populated sections (`action_items`, `decisions`, attributions) without operator intervention.
